handoff_reason_note: First checkpoint of the sprint — schema and workflow scaffolding landed; next session wires up the helper script and tests.
checkpoint_summary: Sprint S-061 introduces a bounded session-handoff workflow so a long Claude Code session can stop at a clean checkpoint and a fresh session can resume the same sprint from a structured artifact. This checkpoint completed the schema, the example file, and the continue-work workflow scaffolding. The helper script and tests are next.

next_actions.title: Implement the close_session.py helper
next_actions.detail: Validate next_session.json against the schema, commit, push, then trigger the continue-work workflow via repository_dispatch when run with --dispatch.

next_actions.title: Add tests/test_session_handoff_validate.py
next_actions.detail: Cover malformed JSON, missing required fields, ready_for_continue=false rejection, and a happy-path validation.

guardrails: Do not modify any code under src/runtime/ or src/units/ in this sprint.
Do not change live-trading flags or strategy parameters.
Do not introduce a database — repo files are the source of truth.
Do not extend this routine into perpetual self-directed planning.
continuation_prompt: You are resuming sprint S-061-session-handoff on branch claude/session-handoff-workflow-00IP8. Read automation/session_handoff/next_session.json first; it lists completed_items, next_actions, files_to_review, and guardrails. Pick up the first entry of next_actions and stop at the next safe checkpoint. Do not start a new sprint.

history.note: Initial example artifact for sprint S-061.
